flags.description: Transform the cycling-training-app from a read-only dashboard into a tool AI agents can leverage for training data retrieval, analysis, and coaching recommendations. Core goal: reliable Intervals.icu data access piped through LLM reasoning for personalized fitness guidance.

Build a robust CLI script/module that fetches training data from Intervals.icu using HTTP Basic Auth (username=API_KEY format). Must handle the STRAVA activity limitation gracefully and pull full activity details (power, HR, elevation, TSS, PMC) for non-STRAVA activities. Output structured JSON for downstream use.

Design prompt templates that take structured training data (PMC metrics, activity history, weekly volume, FTP zones, workout types) and produce professional coaching recommendations. Templates must cover: daily workout prescription, weekly training plan, form/fatigue assessment, periodization advice.

Build REST API endpoints designed for AI agent consumption (not browser HTML) that expose: GET /api/coaching/daily-recommendation, POST /api/coaching/analyze-activity, GET /api/training/pmc. Return structured JSON with all metrics an LLM needs to reason about training load and form.

Implement heuristics + LLM-based classification to categorize activities by type (endurance, threshold, recovery, race, base, commute) and detect training patterns: back-to-back hard days, insufficient recovery, training load spikes, volume trends.

Build a long-term planning module that takes a target event/date and creates a periodized training plan (base → build → peak → race → recovery) with weekly TSS targets, workout type distribution, and FTP progression estimates. Integrate with the LLM coaching engine for adjustments.

Create a CLI command (e.g. 'td workout' or a Python script) that: (1) pulls latest data from Intervals.icu, (2) pipes it through the LLM coaching prompt, (3) outputs a formatted daily/weekly workout recommendation. One-command execution for quick agent access.

Build a module that packages the user's recent training data into a compact, LLM-friendly format (markdown or structured text) optimized for injection into an AI agent's system prompt. Includes: PMC snapshot, recent ride summaries, power zones, HR zones, FTP, form trends.
